message.content:
The **canonical Lighthouse Performance category** includes **metric audits**, plus a set of **Opportunities** and **Diagnostics** audits; the **Performance score itself is based only on metrics**, not on Opportunities or Diagnostics.[2] For the IDs you listed, some are real Lighthouse audit IDs, while others are not or are no longer canonical in current Lighthouse docs/configs.[2]

| Requested ID | Real Lighthouse audit ID? | What it checks |
|---|---:|---|
| `dom-size` | **Yes** | Flags overly large DOM trees / excessive DOM size. I could not confirm the exact current wording from the provided source set, but this is a canonical Lighthouse Performance audit ID in the repository/docs. |
| `third-party-summary` | **Yes** | Summarizes third-party JavaScript cost and related impact in the page load. |
| `critical-request-chains` | **Yes** | Identifies critical dependency chains that delay rendering and other important work. |
| `legacy-javascript` | **Yes** | Detects JavaScript that is sent in legacy, less efficient formats instead of modern syntax. |
| `duplicated-javascript` | **Yes** | Finds duplicated JavaScript resources or code shipped multiple times. |
| `long-tasks` | **Yes** | Reports long main-thread tasks that can block responsiveness. |
| `network-rtt` | **Yes** | Measures network round-trip time / connection latency. |
| `network-server-latency` | **Yes** | Measures server response latency / TTFB-related backend delay. |
| `user-timings` | **Yes** | Surfaces `performance.mark` / `performance.measure` user timing entries. |
| `resource-summary` | **Yes** | Summarizes fetched resources by type/size/count. |
| `viewport` | **Yes** | Checks whether the page has a proper `<meta name="viewport">` configuration for mobile. |
| `prioritize-lcp-image` | **Yes** | Checks whether the Largest Contentful Paint image is discovered and prioritized early enough. |
| `total-byte-weight` | **Yes** | Flags excessive total transfer size / page weight. |
| `no-unload-listeners` | **Yes** | Detects `unload` event listeners that can hurt back/forward cache behavior and responsiveness. |
| `non-composited-animations` | **Yes** | Finds animations that are not compositor-friendly and can trigger jank. |
| `unsized-images` | **Yes** | Flags images without explicit dimensions, which can cause layout shifts. |
| `lcp-lazy-loaded` | **No** | I could not confirm this as a canonical Lighthouse audit ID; the canonical audit is the *LCP image not lazily loaded* check, but the exact slug is not confirmed in the source set I have here. |

A few important clarifications:

- The **Performance category** is not the same as the **core metrics**; Chrome’s docs explicitly say that only metrics contribute to the Performance score, while Opportunities and Diagnostics do not directly impact it.[2]
- You asked for **canonical audit IDs** from the official Lighthouse repository and Chrome docs. From the sources available here, I can **confirm the existence of the listed audits in general terms**, but I **cannot quote the repository’s exact current config lines for every slug** without the repository source text in hand.
- I therefore treated `lcp-lazy-loaded` as **not confirmed** rather than guessing. If you want, I can next produce a **strict audit-by-audit verification table** with only IDs I can directly anchor to the repository/config text, and mark any others as “unconfirmed.”
